source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Identify cryptographic algorithms and cipher suites in use across protocols, applications, and infrastructure to enable transitions.
evidence_quote: Security protocols include a mechanism to identify the algorithm or cipher suite in use. An algorithm identifier might be explicitly carried in the protocol, a management mechanism can be used to identify the algorithm, or the algorithm might be implied by the protocol version number.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Establish local policies to select algorithms and limit allowable combinations during session establishment.
evidence_quote: Local policy can limit the allowable combinations. ... Local policy may select an algorithm other than the mandatory-to-implement one.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Develop a strategic plan to manage cryptographic risks and integrate crypto agility into the organization's risk management framework.
evidence_quote: Executives can leverage the insights in this paper to develop a comprehensive strategic and tactical plan that integrates crypto agility into the organization’s overall risk management framework...

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Utilize certificate expiration and revocation mechanisms to enforce the transition away from vulnerable algorithms.
evidence_quote: A cryptographic key is associated with a particular algorithm, which means that key expiration and revocation are important tools for cryptographic algorithm transition.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Configure systems to prohibit the use of specific vulnerable algorithms to force transitions.
evidence_quote: Additionally, administrators can change a configuration setting to prohibit the use of a particular algorithm as a means of forcing algorithm transition.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Implement mechanisms to determine when deployed implementations have shifted from old algorithms to new ones.
evidence_quote: Implementations should also provide a way to determine when deployed implementations have shifted from the old algorithms to more desirable ones.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Monitor cryptographic research results to discover new attacks and assess impacts on existing protocols.
evidence_quote: Monitoring cryptographic research results provides a way to discover new attacks, assess impacts to existing security protocols, and foresee needed changes.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Ensure algorithm negotiation mechanisms include integrity protection to prevent downgrade attacks.
evidence_quote: The mechanism that a security protocol uses to perform cryptographic algorithm negotiation should include integrity protection. If the integrity of algorithm selection during negotiation is not protected, the protocol will be subject to a downgrade attack...

source_name: HKMA Fintech Promotion Blueprint (Fintech 2030) — Feb 2026
requirement: Establish a governance framework for DLT adoption including asset classification and network architecture standards.
evidence_quote: Overcoming these barriers requires industry‑wide coordination... establishing clear standards for asset classification, governance framework, and preferred DLT network architectures.

source_name: HKMA Fintech Promotion Blueprint (Fintech 2030) — Feb 2026
requirement: Develop a cybersecurity baseline to govern due diligence processes for fintech technology service providers.
evidence_quote: Developing a cybersecurity baseline for fintech services can help banks streamline their due diligence processes for technology service providers.

source_name: HKMA Fintech Promotion Blueprint (Fintech 2030) — Feb 2026
requirement: Implement robust governance mechanisms to manage third-party risks associated with A.I., DLT, and HPC implementations.
evidence_quote: Insecure third-party services and weak governance mechanisms can further amplify these risks... promotional priorities will focus on advancing robust governance...

source_name: HKMA Fintech Promotion Blueprint (Fintech 2030) — Feb 2026
requirement: Conduct a baseline assessment of strategic awareness and operational readiness for post-quantum cryptography adoption.
evidence_quote: The initiative will begin with a baseline Quantum Preparedness Assessment, which will measure strategic awareness and operational readiness...

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Identify the set of cryptographic assets and processes in the system to compile a comprehensive inventory before planning migration.
evidence_quote: Thus the first stage of migration is to identify the set of cryptographic assets and processes in the system (the inventory).

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Clearly indicate dependencies and the liable party for assets not under organizational control but required for migration.
evidence_quote: If assets identified in the inventory are not in the control of the organization... the dependency, including the party liable to assure migration of the asset, should be clearly indicated in the inventory.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Include identification of the root of trust for assets relying on specific trust or credential management frameworks.
evidence_quote: For such entities that rely on specific roots of trust, the inventory should include identification of the root of trust of the asset.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Assess the ability of devices to generate and store key pairs for the Quantum Safe Cryptography solution.
evidence_quote: As part of the inventory... there should be an assessment of the ability of devices (acting on behalf of the principal) to generate and store a key pair for the Quantum Safe Cryptography solution

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Appoint a single manager with responsibility for compiling the inventory who reports to the migration planning manager.
evidence_quote: Appointment of a migration inventory manager: A single manager should be appointed with responsibility for compiling the inventory. The migration inventory manager should report to the migration planning manager.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Integrate migration roles into the existing organization to ensure it is recognized as a board-level strategic activity.
evidence_quote: The roles for migration identified here... should be integrated to the existing organization such that it is clear the migration is a board level activity (i.e. senior strategic management).

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Allocate budget for inventory compilation acknowledging potential significant financial and organizational costs.
evidence_quote: Allocation of budget for inventory compilation: The compilation of the inventory can incur significant cost (financial, temporal, organizational and for technical provisions) if no equivalent inventory already exists.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Create a migration plan that includes a full inventory and specifies whether each asset will be migrated or retired.
evidence_quote: The migration plan should include the following: A full inventory of assets... For each asset: Whether a given asset will be migrated.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Define an orderly sequence for migrating inter-dependent assets based on cryptographic relationships identified in the inventory.
evidence_quote: An orderly sequence of migration of inter-dependent assets... When, and in what order, assets are migrated depends on an understanding of their cryptographic relationship and any dependencies

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Plan for new Public Key Certificates containing Quantum Safe public keys for every entity in the PKI.
evidence_quote: For any PKI in the inventory... every entity in the PKI will need new PKCs that contain Quantum Safe public keys.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Ensure the migration plan addresses support for both classical and Quantum Safe algorithms if backwards compatibility is required.
evidence_quote: If backwards compatibility is required during a phased migration, then the application will have to support both classical and Quantum Safe algorithms.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Identify PKI entities unable to handle Quantum Safe primitives and mark them for replacement.
evidence_quote: The planning should identify the ability of any PKI entity to handle certificates containing the larger Quantum Safe public keys and signatures. If any PKI entity cannot handle QSC primitives these entities should be marked for replacement.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Consider provisions for cryptographic agility in new or updated PKI entities to allow switching algorithms if vulnerabilities are found.
evidence_quote: Provisions for cryptographic agility should be considered for any new or updated entities in the PKI: If a vulnerability is found in the Quantum Safe algorithm, it may be necessary to switch to a different Quantum Safe algorithm entirely

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Include dependency testing in the migration plan to verify inter-asset relationships.
evidence_quote: The migration plan should stress the role of dependency testing.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Migrate Hardware Based Security Environments to support Quantum Safe algorithms before migrating dependent assets.
evidence_quote: If any asset to be migrated is dependent on an HBSE, then that HBSE should be migrated to ensure it supports the algorithm and cryptographic key requirements of the migrated asset before the depending asset is migrated.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Maintain security state during migration by not removing non-Quantum Safe encryption prior to imposing Quantum Safe encryption.
evidence_quote: Where the base state is that all data is encrypted... the non-QSC encryption should not be removed (i.e. go clear) prior to imposing the QSC based encryption mode.

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028

evidence_quote: the government intends to enact a dedicated law that regulates national cyber security efforts. The law will define which organization qualifies as an 'essential organization' in terms of cyber and stipulate the obligations applicable to them – the foremost being the obligation to manage cyber risks effectively

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028

evidence_quote: Israel will promote the formulation of a comprehensive national policy that defines a normative framework for assuring the digital identity of users and organizations, using user-centric principles. This policy will clarify the government's role regarding standards

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028
requirement: Embed cybersecurity requirements in government tenders and contracts starting from the planning phase.
evidence_quote: The Government will incorporate appropriate cybersecurity requirements in government tenders and contracts for the products and services it procures, starting from the planning and initiation phase.

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028
requirement: Define sectoral regulator powers to supervise and enforce cyber security provisions for essential organizations.
evidence_quote: the proposed national legislation will define the powers of the sectoral regulators to equip them with the appropriate tools to supervise and enforce the implementation of the provisions of such law by these essential organizations.

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028
requirement: Mandate reporting of certain cyber-attacks by essential organizations to national authorities.
evidence_quote: The law will define which organization qualifies as an 'essential organization' in terms of cyber and stipulate the obligations applicable to them – the foremost being the obligation to manage cyber risks effectively and the obligation to report certain cyber-attacks.

source_name: Israel National Cybersecurity Strategy 2025-2028
requirement: Establish a national system for reporting all types of cyber-attacks with user-friendly online access.
evidence_quote: a national system will be established for reporting of all types of cyber-attacks, with user-friendly online access that ensures privacy protection. This system will help increase report rates of cyber-attacks

requirement: Enforce a policy prohibiting new classical-only cryptographic deployments.

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force
requirement: Adopt crypto agility as a guiding principle for cryptographic transitions.

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force
requirement: Prioritize procurement of indigenously developed quantum-safe solutions.

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force

source_name: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force
requirement: Upgrade PKI, HSMs, KMS, and libraries to PQC-ready versions by 2030.

requirement: Contain classical-only systems within controlled enclaves where immediate migration is not feasible.

requirement: Assign unique identifiers to each software component to maintain linkage during ownership or branding changes.
evidence_quote: Assign unique identifiers to each component: ...To address this, a unique identifier should be created. This identifier may follow the following structure...

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)
requirement: Create a comprehensive inventory of all cryptographic assets including algorithms, keys, ownership, usage, and lifecycle metadata.
evidence_quote: Financial Institutions should create a comprehensive inventory of all cryptographic assets within their organisation. This involves identifying where cryptography (encryption, digital signature) is used across data-in-transit, data-at-rest, and data-in-use. The inventory should not only track cryptographic algorithm and keys, but also capture metadata like ownership, usage, and lifecycle manageme…

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)
requirement: Conduct risk assessments to evaluate quantum vulnerability of cryptographic assets considering data longevity and migration timelines.
evidence_quote: Next, a risk assessment should be conducted to evaluate the quantum vulnerability of each cryptographic asset. This process should account for variability in timelines across different algorithms... Factors to consider include the shelf life of encrypted data and the timeline for quantum computers becoming capable of breaking current cryptographic algorithms.

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)
requirement: Prioritize remediation for systems protecting long-lived sensitive data or those with critical operational dependencies.
evidence_quote: Given that the timelines for quantum threats may differ significantly across algorithms, high-risk areas where data has long-term value or where cryptographic mechanisms are critical to operations should be prioritised. Systems that protect long-lived, sensitive data or have extensive third party dependencies may also require more immediate attention.

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)
requirement: Implement crypto-agile systems capable of adapting to future cryptographic requirements and quantum threats.
evidence_quote: Additionally, financial institutions must ensure they have crypto-agile systems that can quickly adapt to future cryptographic requirements and quantum threat. Collaboration with vendors and implementing a phased approach for integrating PQC will be key to minimising disruptions while securing critical systems against future quantum threats.

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)
requirement: Assess vendor readiness for PQC standards and incorporate PQC requirements into new contracts and service-level agreements.
evidence_quote: Firms should assess each vendor's cryptographic protocols, their ability to support new PQC standards, and their readiness to adopt quantum-resistant algorithms... Additionally, firms should consider incorporating requirements for PQC in new contracts and service-level agreements to ensure all vendor relationships align with their quantum migration strategies.
evidence_location: Section 6: Vendor Readiness Incorporated Into Planning

source_name: CMORG Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography (UK Financial Sector)

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Designate an Information Officer to oversee compliance with personal information processing conditions and ensure accountability.
evidence_quote: The responsible party must ensure that the conditions set out in this Chapter, and all the measures that give effect to such conditions, are complied with at the time of the determination of the purpose and means of the processing and during the processing itself.

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Maintain documentation of all processing operations under the responsibility of the responsible party.
evidence_quote: A responsible party must maintain the documentation of all processing operations under its responsibility as referred to in section 14 or 51 of the Promotion of Access to Information Act.

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Destroy or delete records of personal information as soon as reasonably practicable after the retention period expires.
evidence_quote: A responsible party must destroy or delete a record of personal information or de-identify it as soon as reasonably practicable after the responsible party is no longer authorised to retain the record in terms of subsection (1) or (2).

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Ensure destruction or deletion of records prevents reconstruction in an intelligible form.
evidence_quote: The destruction or deletion of a record of personal information in terms of subsection (4) must be done in a manner that prevents its reconstruction in an intelligible form.

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Restrict processing of personal information when accuracy is contested or for legal proof purposes.
evidence_quote: The responsible party must restrict processing of personal information if its accuracy is contested by the data subject, for a period enabling the responsible party to verify the accuracy of the information; or the responsible party no longer needs the personal information for achieving the purpose for which the information was collected or subsequently processed, but it has to be maintained for…

source_name: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA, Act 4 of 2013)
requirement: Notify the Information Regulator and data subjects of security compromises involving unauthorized access or acquisition.
evidence_quote: A data subject has the right to be notified that his, her or its personal information has been accessed or acquired by an unauthorised person as provided for in terms of section 22.

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)

evidence_quote: A data controller or data processor may designate or appoint a data protection officer... where the core activities of the data controller or data processor consist of processing of sensitive categories of personal data.

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Document and publish contact details of the Data Protection Officer on the website and communicate them to the Data Commissioner.
evidence_quote: A data controller or data processor shall publish the contact details of the data protection officer on the website and communicate them to the Data Commissioner who shall ensure that the same information is available on the official website.

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Register with the Data Commissioner and provide a general description of risks, safeguards, and security measures to ensure data protection.
evidence_quote: An application... shall provide... a general description of the risks, safeguards, security measures and mechanisms to ensure the protection of personal data

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment for high-risk processing operations to identify risks and define security measures.
evidence_quote: Where a processing operation is likely to result in high risk... a data controller or data processor shall... carry out a data protection impact assessment... including... the measures envisaged to address the risks and the safeguards, security measures and mechanisms

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Submit to periodical audits by the Data Commissioner to verify compliance with data protection processes and systems.
evidence_quote: The Data Commissioner may carry out periodical audits of the processes and systems of the data controllers or data processors to ensure compliance with this Act.

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Maintain a register of data controllers and processors as a public document available for inspection to verify registration status.
evidence_quote: The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain a register of the registered data controllers and data processors... The register shall be a public document and available for inspection by any person.

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Notify the Data Commissioner of any changes to registration particulars to ensure the register remains accurate and up-to-date.
evidence_quote: A data controller or data processor shall notify the Data Commissioner of a change in any particular outlined under subsection (2).

source_name: Kenya Data Protection Act (No. 24 of 2019)
requirement: Limit the retention of personal data to no longer than necessary for the purposes for which it was collected.

source_name: PCI DSS Quick Reference Guide v3 (v4.0 overview)
requirement: Follow change control processes and procedures for all changes to system components.

source_name: PCI DSS Quick Reference Guide v3 (v4.0 overview)

source_name: PCI DSS Quick Reference Guide v3 (v4.0 overview)

source_name: PCI DSS Quick Reference Guide v3 (v4.0 overview)

source_name: PCI DSS Quick Reference Guide v3 (v4.0 overview)

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Identify quantum-vulnerable cryptographic standards and the post-quantum standards to which products must transition.
evidence_quote: It identifies existing quantum-vulnerable cryptographic standards and the quantum-resistant standards to which information technology products and services will need to transition.

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Plan for the adoption of new PQC algorithms and the deprecation and removal of quantum-vulnerable algorithms.
evidence_quote: This transition will involve the adoption of new PQC algorithms as well as the careful deprecation, controlled legacy use, and eventual removal of quantum-vulnerable algorithms that are currently widespread in technological infrastructures.

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Update software cryptographic libraries to incorporate standardized PQC algorithms and ensure security against side-channel attacks.
evidence_quote: Updating them ensures that developers have access to quantum-resistant cryptographic functions without implementing complex algorithms themselves. This transition involves adding new algorithms, optimizing their implementations for performance, and ensuring those implementations are secure against side-channel attacks.

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Modify IT applications to support PQC algorithms for encryption, signatures, and key exchange, including refactoring code and testing.
evidence_quote: Applications and services must be modified to support PQC algorithms for encryption, digital signatures, and key exchange. This requires updating the underlying cryptographic implementations... Developers need to refactor code, conduct extensive testing...

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Update PKI components to issue and manage certificates using PQC algorithms and modify validation mechanisms.
evidence_quote: PKI components must be updated to issue, distribute, and manage certificates that use PQC algorithms and to sign certificates and revocation status information using PQC algorithms. This includes supporting new cryptographic algorithms in certificate issuance processes and modifying validation and revocation mechanisms.

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Upgrade cryptographic hardware modules to support PQC algorithms with larger key sizes and different computational requirements.
evidence_quote: Hardware modules must be upgraded or redesigned to support PQC algorithms, which often have larger key sizes and different computational requirements. This includes updating firmware or hardware to handle new algorithms...

source_name: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards
requirement: Consider hybrid solutions combining quantum-resistant and vulnerable algorithms as a temporary measure during transition.
evidence_quote: The migration to post-quantum cryptography may initially include hybrid solutions that incorporate the use of quantum-resistant and quantum-vulnerable algorithms... However, hybrid solutions add complexity... typically expected to be temporary measures...

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Assess and evaluate available technologies to understand the crypto estate before transitioning.
evidence_quote: It requires a combination of engineering efforts, proactive assessment and evaluation of available technologies, and a careful approach to product development and deployment.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Plan for staged migrations where upgraded agents coexist with non-upgraded agents.
evidence_quote: Such transitions are some of the most complicated in the tech industry and will require staged migrations in which upgraded agents need to co-exist and communicate with non-upgraded agents at a scale never before undertaken.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Minimize the transition timeframe to reduce the security gap where data remains vulnerable.
evidence_quote: The model essentially asks how one is preparing IT systems during those 'y' years (in other words, how one can minimize those 'y' years) to minimize the transition phase to a PQC infrastructure and hence minimize the risks of data being exposed in the future.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Consider long lifetimes for authentication use-cases such as firmware, legal documents, and embedded certificates.
evidence_quote: some authentication use-cases do require long lifetimes, such as signing firmware or software that will be active for decades, signing legal documents, or signing certificates that will be embedded into hardware devices such as smartcards.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Evaluate the actual migration timeline honestly to understand exposure risks.
evidence_quote: Organizations should also consider carefully and honestly what their migration timeline 'y' actually is.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Coordinate transitions between organizations and ecosystems due to the wide-ranging impact of PQC.
evidence_quote: a significant amount of coordination between organizations, and within and between ecosystems needs to be taken into account.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Prepare for significant protocol redesign rather than simple algorithm replacement due to PQC properties.
evidence_quote: This is not merely a one-for-one replacement of algorithms; in many cases, the shift to PQC will involve redesigning protocols and infrastructure to accommodate the significant differences in resource utilization and key sizes between traditional and PQC algorithms.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Evaluate trade-offs between security and performance when implementing post-quantum algorithms.
evidence_quote: Engineers need to understand how to implement post-quantum algorithms in applications, how to evaluate the trade-offs between security and performance, and how to ensure backward compatibility with current systems where needed.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Ensure backward compatibility with current systems during the transition phase.
evidence_quote: Engineers need to understand how to implement post-quantum algorithms in applications, how to evaluate the trade-offs between security and performance, and how to ensure backward compatibility with current systems where needed.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Remain vigilant and prepare early for quantum computing advances that may be ahead of public research.
evidence_quote: it is to the industry's benefit to remain vigilant and prepare as early as possible. Bear in mind also that while the industry tracks advances from public research institutions... there is also a great deal of large-budget quantum research being conducted privately by various national interests.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Address the 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat by implementing quantum-safe strategies immediately.
evidence_quote: This implies that, every day, sensitive encrypted data is susceptible to the attack by not implementing quantum-safe strategies, as it corresponds to data possibly being deciphered in the future.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Recognize that current best practices for side-channel resistance may be insufficient against quantum adversaries.
evidence_quote: implementers of cryptographic hardware should be aware that current best-practices for side-channel resistance may not be sufficient against quantum adversaries.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Replace traditional algorithms with either PQC KEMs or PQC signatures based on their specific function.
evidence_quote: When upgrading protocols, it is important to replace the existing use of traditional algorithms with either a PQC KEM or a PQC signature method, depending on how the traditional algorithm was previously being used.

source_name: Post-Quantum Cryptography for Engineers
requirement: Account for API differences when incorporating KEMs into protocols or applications.
evidence_quote: Additionally, KEMs, as described in Section 9, present a different API than either key agreement or key transport primitives. As a result, they may require protocol-level or application-level changes in order to be incorporated.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Validate cryptographic modules against FIPS 140-3 using accredited laboratories to ensure conformance before deployment.
evidence_quote: The Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) validates cryptographic modules to Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) 140-3... In the CMVP, vendors of cryptographic modules use independent, accredited Cryptographic and Security Testing (CST) laboratories to have their modules tested.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Employ only approved security functions specified in FIPS or NIST Special Publications for protecting sensitive information.
evidence_quote: Cryptographic modules that conform to this standard shall employ Approved security functions such as cryptographic algorithms, cryptographic key management techniques, and authentication techniques that have been approved for protecting Federal government sensitive information.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Plan for the transition to FIPS 140-3 compliant modules within six months of standard approval.
evidence_quote: Agencies should develop plans for the acquisition of products that are compliant with FIPS 140-3; however, agencies may purchase any of the products on the CMVP validated modules list.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Ensure the responsible authority in each agency verifies that module security is sufficient and acceptable for the protected information.
evidence_quote: The operator of a cryptographic module is responsible for ensuring that the security provided by a module is sufficient and acceptable to the owner of the information that is being protected... The responsible authority in each agency shall ensure that the security of the system is sufficient and acceptable.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Acknowledge and accept residual risk when conformance to the standard alone is insufficient to ensure module security.
evidence_quote: While the security requirements specified in this standard are intended to maintain the security provided by a cryptographic module, conformance to this standard is not sufficient to ensure that a particular module is secure... any residual risk is acknowledged and accepted.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography

evidence_quote: The FIPS 140-3 IUT submission (e.g., Security Policy and validation test report) shall precisely identify the EVM by name, CMVP certificate number and version(s).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Mark all references to embedded module functionality with [EVM] in Security Policy tables to distinguish IUT from EVM services.
evidence_quote: This shall be done per below by marking '[EVM]' to the start of each applicable reference within the following WebCyptik tables... All other areas of the Security Policy where EVM information is needed that are not shown in the above tables, shall also be marked with [EVM].

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Ensure the embedded validated module (EVM) status is Active at the time of IUT submission to CMVP.
evidence_quote: The EVM status shall be Active (i.e., is not on the Historical or Revoked list) at the time of the IUT submission to the CMVP.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Inherit the Historical validation status if the embedded validated module moves to the historical list for any reason.
evidence_quote: The IUT shall inherit the Historical validation status if the EVM moves to the historical list for any reason (e.g., is sunset or an algorithm transition).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Only claim EVM services or algorithms as approved if they were approved at the time of IUT submission.
evidence_quote: To claim an EVM's implementation as an approved service / algorithm within the IUT documentation, the IUT shall only use EVM services / algorithms that are approved at the time of the IUT submission to the CMVP

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Verify that the operational environment of the embedded algorithm implementation matches the module's tested environment.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography

requirement: Enforce TLS 1.2 deprecation by end of 2031 and migrate to TLS 1.3 or quantum-safe mechanisms.
evidence_quote: TLS 1.2 is only recommended until the end of 2031, since, based on current knowledge, no quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms will be standardized for TLS 1.2.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Discontinue use of DSA and DHE cipher suites by end of 2029.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Discontinue RSA signatures with PKCS #1 v1.5 padding by end of 2025.
evidence_quote: The use of the signature algorithm rsa (IANA no. 1) is recommended only up to 2025, because it uses the PKCS #1 v1.5 padding scheme

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS

evidence_quote: If a cipher suite with Perfect Forward Secrecy is used, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware such as HSMs or chip cards.
evidence_quote: Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable TLS data compression to prevent CRIME side-channel attacks.
evidence_quote: If this cannot be ensured, it is recommended not to use TLS data compression.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable the Heartbeat extension to prevent memory disclosure vulnerabilities.
evidence_quote: It is recommended not to use the Heartbeat extension.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Encrypt-then-MAC extension to mitigate padding oracle attacks.
evidence_quote: The use of the TLS extension 'encrypt-then-MAC' according to [RFC 7366] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Extended Master Secret extension to prevent triple handshake attacks.
evidence_quote: Using the TLS extension Extended Master Secret according to [RFC 7627] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory key lengths ensuring minimum 3000 bits for RSA/DSA and 250 bits for ECDSA/ECDH.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define SA lifetimes based on security requirements and enforce rekeying before expiration.
evidence_quote: The SA lifetime should be defined depending on the security requirements of the application. This applies to both IKE-SAs and IPsec-SAs. In ordinary operating scenarios, the IKE-SA lifetime should not exceed 24 h and the IPsec-SA lifetime should not exceed 4 h.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Renegotiate security associations after the defined period of time or data volume limit has expired.
evidence_quote: Both an IKE-SA and an IPsec-SA should only be valid for a limited period of time and renegotiated after this period of time has expired. As an alternative, the transmitted data volume can also be used as criterion for the renegotiation of an IPsec-SA.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Irrevocably delete ephemeral keys immediately after use and ensure no copies are made.
evidence_quote: If an IKEv2/IPsec connection is secured by means of an encryption algorithm, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware to prevent copying, misuse, and manipulation.
evidence_quote: Private cryptographic keys, especially static keys and signature keys, must be stored and processed in a secure manner. This includes, among other things, the protection against copying, misuse and manipulation of the keys. Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Migrate from sole use of classical key agreement mechanisms to hybrid quantum-safe mechanisms by end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031... For quantum-safe key agreement, the BSI intends to recommend the mechanisms ml-kem-768 and ml-kem-1024... combined with a key exchange mechanism from Table 6 (hybrid).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
