evidence_quote: 11.3 Health Testing... 11.3.2 Testing the Instantiate Function... 11.3.3 Testing the Generate Function... 11.3.4 Testing the Reseed Function... 11.3.5 Testing the Uninstantiate Function

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Define and handle errors encountered during normal operation and health testing to ensure system integrity.
evidence_quote: 11.4 Error Handling... 11.4.1 Errors Encountered During Normal Operation... 11.4.2 Errors Encountered During Health Testing

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Reseed the DRBG mechanism to acquire additional bits that affect the internal state before the seedlife expires.
evidence_quote: Reseed: To acquire additional bits that will affect the internal state of the DRBG mechanism.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Uninstantiate the DRBG to terminate the instantiation and securely clear internal state.
evidence_quote: Uninstantiate: The termination of a DRBG instantiation.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Ensure conformance testing for DRBG implementations is conducted within the CMVP and CAVP frameworks.
evidence_quote: Conformance testing for implementations of this Recommendation will be conducted within the framework of the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) and the Cryptographic Algorithm Validation Program (CAVP).

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Submit entropy sources to NVLAP-accredited laboratories for validation testing against SP 800-90B requirements before deployment.
evidence_quote: Validation consists of testing by an NVLAP-accredited laboratory against the requirements of SP 800-90B, followed by a review of the results by CAVP and CMVP.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Collect a sequential dataset of at least 1,000,000 raw samples from the noise source for validation testing.
evidence_quote: A sequential dataset of at least 1 000 000 sample values obtained directly from the noise source (i.e., raw data) shall be collected for validation.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Collect a conditioned sequential dataset of at least 1,000,000 consecutive outputs if a non-listed conditioning component is used.
evidence_quote: If the entropy source includes a conditioning component that is not listed in Section 3.1.5.1.1, a conditioned sequential dataset of at least 1 000 000 consecutive conditioning component outputs shall be collected for validation.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Perform restart tests by restarting the entropy source 1000 times and collecting 1000 consecutive samples for each restart.
evidence_quote: For the restart tests (see Section 3.1.4), the entropy source must be restarted 1000 times; for each restart, 1000 consecutive samples shall be collected directly from the noise source.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Provide rationale and analysis to support any claim that the noise source generates Independent and Identically Distributed (IID) samples.
evidence_quote: The submitter makes an IID claim on the noise source, based on the submitter’s analysis of the design. The submitter shall provide rationale for the IID claim.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Provide an initial entropy estimate for the noise source outputs based on the submitter's analysis of the design.
evidence_quote: The submitter shall provide an entropy estimate for the noise source outputs, which is based on the submitter’s analysis of the noise source.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Implement health tests to ensure the noise source and entropy source continue to operate as expected and detect failures quickly.
evidence_quote: Health tests are an integral part of the entropy source design that are intended to ensure that the noise source and the entire entropy source continue to operate as expected. When testing the entropy source, the end goal is to obtain assurance that failures of the entropy source are caught quickly and with a high probability.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Include health tests capable of detecting likely failure modes for the entropy source and specifically the noise source.
evidence_quote: Another aspect of health testing strategy is determining the likely failure modes for the entropy source and, in particular, for the noise source. Health tests are expected to include tests that can detect these failure conditions.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Categorize health tests into start-up, continuous, and on-demand tests to monitor entropy source status.
evidence_quote: The health tests can be separated into three categories: start-up tests, continuous tests (primarily on the noise source), and on-demand tests.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Ensure the entropy source design accounts for variations in noise source behavior that may affect the entropy rate of the output.
evidence_quote: The developer should consider how the conditioning component to be used and how variations in the behavior of the noise source may affect the entropy rate of the output.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Validate RBG constructions against SP 800-90C using accredited testing labs to ensure conformance with specified requirements.
evidence_quote: The term 'shall' is used when a requirement is testable by a testing lab during implementation validation using operational tests or a code review.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Ensure RBG constructions use entropy sources validated for compliance with SP 800-90B or SP 800-90C.
evidence_quote: Each RBG includes a DRBG from SP 800-90A and is based on the use of a randomness source that is validated for compliance with SP 800-90B or SP 800-90C.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Implement health testing for RBG components to detect failures in entropy sources and non-entropy-source components.
evidence_quote: Section 8 discusses health and implementation validation testing. ... Handling Failures ... Entropy-Source Failures ... Failures by Non-Entropy-Source Components

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Define and enforce reseed procedures for RBG constructions to maintain security strength over time.
evidence_quote: Sections 4, 5, 6, and 7 specify the RBG constructions... Reseeding an RBG2 Construction... Reseeding the DRBG Instantiation...

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Verify that RBG constructions meet minimum entropy requirements for seed material to ensure cryptographic security.
evidence_quote: SP 800-90C requires security_strength/2 bits of randomness to be obtained from a randomness source in place of a nonce in addition to the randomness required to establish a DRBG's security strength.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Establish and enforce documented policies for scheme operations, ensuring adherence to CCRA and ISO/IEC 17065 requirements.
evidence_quote: NIAP will: Establish and implement policies and procedures for the operation of the scheme, and ensure the policies and procedures are adhered to

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Define and document specific roles and responsibilities for NIAP management positions including Director, Quality Manager, and Technical Lead.
evidence_quote: NIAP management functions are comprised of the following positions: Director, Deputy Director, Technical Lead, Quality Manager, Technical Oversight Team, Business Manager, Contracts Manager, and Data/Records Manager.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Implement a formal Quality Policy endorsed by upper-level management to ensure impartiality and consistency in evaluations.
evidence_quote: A Quality Policy defines the overall intentions and direction of an organization with regard to quality and is endorsed and expressed by upper-level management.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Maintain a documented Records Management Policy ensuring traceability and reproducibility of all evaluation and validation activities.
evidence_quote: NIAP is responsible for maintaining accurate records to demonstrate NIAP procedures have been effectively implemented and to ensure the traceability, repeatability, and reproducibility of evaluations and validations.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Enforce adherence to policies and procedures as a condition of employment or contract for all personnel and contractors.
evidence_quote: All employees, contractors, and others that provide services to NIAP are bound by the policies, procedures, and other conditions set by NIAP... Adherence to these policies and procedures is a condition of employment.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Establish a formal process for handling complaints and appeals regarding NIAP decisions or actions.
evidence_quote: Persons or organizations affected by NIAP decisions and actions have the right to file a formal complaint and appeal the decision when they believe NIAP actions have not been conducted according to the rules or standards of NIAP

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Conduct annual management reviews of the quality system and policy to ensure continued effectiveness.
evidence_quote: NIAP management will perform a formal evaluation of the NIAP quality system and quality policy once per year, at a minimum.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Implement internal audit schedules to verify compliance with quality system procedures and document findings.
evidence_quote: The Quality Manager, or designee, will develop and update an internal audit schedule to perform an audit of each quality system function. The purpose of the audit is to verify NIAP continues to comply with its quality system procedures.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Require all personnel performing work for NIAP to sign non-disclosure agreements regarding proprietary information.
evidence_quote: All persons performing work for NIAP are required to sign non-disclosure agreements avowing they understand and will comply with the proprietary information confidentiality policy described above

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Maintain a Product Compliant List (PCL) of all successfully evaluated and validated products with their Security Targets.
evidence_quote: Publish and maintain a Product Compliant List (PCL) of all successfully evaluated and validated products, along with their respective Security Targets (STs) and validation reports

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Monitor the performance of participating Common Criteria Testing Laboratories (CCTLs) for adherence to scheme terms.
evidence_quote: Monitor the performance of participating CCTLs and their adherence to, application of, and interpretation of the CC and the CEM

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Establish procedures for the approval and monitoring of contractors performing validation or technical oversight functions.
evidence_quote: The NIAP Contracts Manager will interface with appropriate NSA Contract Officers and contractors to ensure all contracts, subcontracts, working agreements, and assessments of contractors are properly executed and operating.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Define a progressive transition strategy for cryptographic products to adopt quantum-resistant cryptography.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI encourage toutes les industries à définir une stratégie de transition progressive vers une cryptographie résistante aux attaques quantiques pour les produits cryptographiques concernés.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Include the quantum threat in risk analysis and consider quantum protection measures for relevant cryptographic products.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI encourage toutes les industries à inclure la menace quantique dans leur analyse de risque et à envisager d'inclure des mesures de protection quantique dans les produits cryptographiques concernés.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Mandate hybridation for products requiring post-quantum protection, except for specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI insiste toujours fortement sur la nécessité de l'hybridation partout où une protection post-quantique est nécessaire, à la fois à court et à moyen terme.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Dimension symmetric primitives to ensure post-quantum security levels equivalent to AES-256 and SHA2-384.
evidence_quote: l'ANSSI encourage également à dimensionner les paramètres des primitives symétriques de manière à assurer une sécurité post-quantique conjecturée ; en pratique, au moins le même niveau de sécurité que l'AES-256 pour les algorithmes de chiffrement par bloc et au moins le même niveau de sécurité que SHA2-384 pour les fonctions de hachage.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Avoid modifying normalized parameters for post-quantum algorithms like CRYSTALS-Kyber and CRYSTALS-Dilithium.
evidence_quote: Il est important d'éviter de modifier les paramètres de l'instance normalisée.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Use the highest possible NIST security level (preferably Level 5 or 3) for post-quantum algorithms.
evidence_quote: Nous recommandons d'utiliser le niveau de sécurité NIST le plus élevé possible, de préférence le niveau 5 (équivalent à l'AES-256) ou le niveau 3 (équivalent à l'AES-192).

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Protect the internal state of signature algorithms like XMSS and LMS against integrity attacks and replay.
evidence_quote: L'état interne de l'algorithme de signature est une donnée très critique et doit être protégé en intégrité. Il doit également être protégé contre les attaques par rejeu.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Undergo ANSSI security visa evaluation including mandatory hybridation analysis for post-quantum products.
evidence_quote: Dans la deuxième phase, les tâches d'évaluation cryptographique des visas de sécurité comprennent une analyse de tous les algorithmes cryptographiques, y compris les algorithmes post-quantiques avec une hybridation obligatoire.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Ensure final products implementing post-quantum protection use hybridation unless using specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: Dans ce cas, tout produit qui inclut une protection post-quantique doit mettre en œuvre l'hybridation, sauf dans les rares cas où la protection post-quantique repose uniquement sur des signatures basées sur le hachage comme XMSS, LMS ou SPHINCS+, pour lesquelles l'hybridation est facultative.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Implement hybridation modes using validated standards or well-studied modes with security proofs.
evidence_quote: De manière générale, comme pour toute fonction cryptographique, l'ANSSI recommande d'utiliser des normes ou des modes bien étudiés avec des preuves de sécurité validées.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Prioritize hybrid post-quantum protection for products offering long-term confidentiality beyond 2030.
evidence_quote: L'utilisation d'une protection hybride post-quantique est recommandée en particulier pour les produits de sécurité destinés à offrir une protection durable des informations (jusqu'à après 2030) ou qui seront potentiellement utilisés après 2030 sans mise à jour.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Enforce key re-exchange after one hour or one gigabyte of data transmitted to renew session keys.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to re-exchange the keys according to Chapter 9 in [RFC 4253], i.e. the session keys are renewed after one hour or after one gigabyte has been transmitted (whichever happens first).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Define maximum periods of use for cryptographic mechanisms and discontinue usage after the specified year.
evidence_quote: The recommendations in this Technical Guideline have a maximum period of use. The indication of the year means that the respective mechanism can be used until the end of the year stated.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Plan migration to quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms by the end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031 (see [TR-02102-1], Section 2.1).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Irrevocably delete ephemeral keys after use and ensure no persistent copies are stored.
evidence_quote: If an SSH connection is secured by means of an encryption algorithm, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Validate that random number generators used for keys and signatures belong to approved classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Ensure SSH implementations support only recommended algorithms with high priority and disable weak or obsolete algorithms.
evidence_quote: The application should support the algorithms recommended in this document and the algorithms given in the specification, but be configured in such a way that the recommended (cryptographically strong) algorithms are used with high priority and the (possibly cryptographically weak or obsolete) algorithms from the SSH specification are used with low priority or, if possible, are not used.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Prohibit the use of SSH-1 due to cryptographic vulnerabilities and mandate SSH-2.
evidence_quote: The use of SSH-2 is recommended. Using SSH-1 is not recommended, since this protocol version contains cryptographic vulnerabilities.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Maintain an inventory of supported key exchange methods and their expiration dates to ensure compliance with security levels.
evidence_quote: The following key exchange methods are recommended: ... Use up to 2031

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Catalog encryption algorithms and their validity periods to manage cryptographic estate lifecycle.
evidence_quote: The following encryption algorithms are recommended in this respect: ... Use up to 2032+

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Record signature algorithms for server authentication and their discontinuation dates.
evidence_quote: The following signature algorithms are recommended for server authentication: ... Use up to 2029 / 2032+

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Adopt a security level of 120 bits for all cryptographic mechanisms as defined in organizational policy.
evidence_quote: The security level for all cryptographic mechanisms in this Technical Guideline depends on the security level stated in Section 1.1 in [TR-02102-1] and is 120 bits.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Establish policy requiring secure storage of private keys using certified hardware such as HSMs or chip cards.
evidence_quote: Private cryptographic keys, especially static keys and signature keys, must be stored and processed in a secure manner. ... Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Designate a Chief Information Security Officer responsible for overseeing and implementing the cybersecurity program.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall designate a CISO. The CISO may be employed by the covered entity, one of its affiliates or a third-party service provider.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement and maintain a written cybersecurity policy approved at least annually by senior officers or the governing body.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement and maintain a written policy or policies, approved at least annually by a senior officer or the covered entity's senior governing body for the protection of its information systems and nonpublic information stored on those information systems.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Ensure the senior governing body exercises oversight of cybersecurity risk management and confirms resource allocation.
evidence_quote: The senior governing body of the covered entity shall exercise oversight of the covered entity's cybersecurity risk management... confirming that the covered entity's management has allocated sufficient resources to implement and maintain an effective cybersecurity program.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Require the CISO to report in writing at least annually to the senior governing body on program effectiveness and risks.
evidence_quote: The CISO of each covered entity shall report in writing at least annually to the senior governing body on the covered entity's cybersecurity program, including... overall effectiveness of the covered entity's cybersecurity program; and material cybersecurity risks to the covered entity.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Submit an annual certification of material compliance signed by the highest-ranking executive and the CISO.
evidence_quote: Annually each covered entity shall submit to the superintendent electronically... a written certification that certifies that the covered entity materially complied with the requirements set forth in this Part during the prior calendar year... signed by the covered entity's highest-ranking executive and its CISO.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Maintain records supporting the annual compliance certification for a period of five years.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall maintain for examination and inspection by the department upon request all records, schedules and other documentation and data supporting the certification or acknowledgment for a period of five years.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies for encryption of nonpublic information in transit and at rest, or approved compensating controls.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement a written policy requiring encryption that meets industry standards, to protect nonpublic information held or transmitted by the covered entity both in transit over external networks and at rest.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for asset inventory management including tracking ownership and classification.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures designed to produce and maintain a complete, accurate and documented asset inventory of the covered entity's information systems... track key information for each asset, including... owner; location; classification or sensitivity.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the secure disposal of nonpublic information no longer necessary for business operations.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall include policies and procedures for the secure disposal on a periodic basis of any nonpublic information... that is no longer necessary for business operations or for other legitimate business purposes.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider security including due diligence and risk assessment.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures designed to ensure the security of information systems and nonpublic information that are accessible to, or held by, third-party service providers... address... the identification and risk assessment of third-party service providers; minimum cybersecurity practices required...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for vulnerability management including penetration testing and remediation.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... develop and implement written policies and procedures for vulnerability management that are designed to assess and maintain the effectiveness of its cybersecurity program... conduct, at a minimum: penetration testing... automated scans... timely remediate vulnerabilities.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for access privileges management including periodic review of user access.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... limit user access privileges to information systems that provide access to nonpublic information to only those necessary to perform the user's job... periodically, but at a minimum annually, review all user access privileges and remove or disable accounts and access that are no longer necessary.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for incident response and business continuity management including testing.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall establish written plans that contain proactive measures to investigate and mitigate cybersecurity events... including but not limited to incident response, business continuity and disaster recovery plans... periodically, but at a minimum annually, test its incident response and BCDR plans.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for application security covering secure development practices.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity's cybersecurity program shall include written procedures, guidelines and standards designed to ensure the use of secure development practices for in-house developed applications utilized by the covered entity...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for risk assessment to inform the design of the cybersecurity program.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall conduct a periodic risk assessment... The risk assessment shall be carried out in accordance with written policies and procedures and shall be documented.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for monitoring authorized user activity to detect unauthorized access.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... implement risk-based policies, procedures and controls designed to monitor the activity of authorized users and detect unauthorized access or use of, or tampering with, nonpublic information by such authorized users.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for multi-factor authentication for remote access and privileged accounts.
evidence_quote: Multi-factor authentication shall be utilized for any individual accessing any information systems of a covered entity... unless the covered entity qualifies for a limited exemption... in which case multi-factor authentication shall be utilized for: remote access... all privileged accounts other than service accounts...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for cybersecurity awareness training updated to reflect identified risks.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... provide periodic, but at a minimum annual, cybersecurity awareness training that includes social engineering for all personnel that is updated to reflect risks identified by the covered entity in its risk assessment.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for audit trails to detect and respond to cybersecurity events.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall securely maintain systems that... include audit trails designed to detect and respond to cybersecurity events that have a reasonable likelihood of materially harming any material part of the normal operations of the covered entity.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Maintain records of audit trails for not fewer than three years.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... maintain records required by paragraph (a)(2) of this section for not fewer than three years.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Maintain records of material financial transactions for not fewer than five years.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall maintain records required by paragraph (a)(1) of this section for not fewer than five years...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for password management meeting industry standards.
evidence_quote: To the extent passwords are employed as a method of authentication, the covered entity shall implement a written password policy that meets industry standards.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the use of qualified cybersecurity personnel sufficient to manage risks.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... utilize qualified cybersecurity personnel of the covered entity, an affiliate or a third-party service provider sufficient to manage the covered entity's cybersecurity risks...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for providing cybersecurity updates and training to personnel.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... provide cybersecurity personnel with cybersecurity updates and training sufficient to address relevant cybersecurity risks...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for verifying that key cybersecurity personnel maintain current knowledge of threats.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... verify that key cybersecurity personnel take steps to maintain current knowledge of changing cybersecurity threats and countermeasures.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the identification and risk assessment of third-party service providers.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures designed to ensure the security of information systems and nonpublic information that are accessible to, or held by, third-party service providers... address... the identification and risk assessment of third-party service providers...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for due diligence processes to evaluate third-party cybersecurity practices.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures... address... due diligence processes used to evaluate the adequacy of cybersecurity practices of such third-party service providers...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for periodic assessment of third-party service providers based on risk.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures... address... periodic assessment of such third-party service providers based on the risk they present and the continued adequacy of their cybersecurity practices.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider access controls including multi-factor authentication.
evidence_quote: Such policies and procedures shall include relevant guidelines... addressing... the third-party service provider's policies and procedures for access controls, including its use of multi-factor authentication as required by section 500.12...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider encryption of nonpublic information.
evidence_quote: Such policies and procedures shall include relevant guidelines... addressing... the third-party service provider's policies and procedures for use of encryption as required by section 500.15...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider notice of cybersecurity events.
evidence_quote: Such policies and procedures shall include relevant guidelines... addressing... notice to be provided to the covered entity in the event of a cybersecurity event directly impacting the covered entity's information systems...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider representations and warranties regarding cybersecurity.
evidence_quote: Such policies and procedures shall include relevant guidelines... addressing... representations and warranties addressing the third-party service provider's cybersecurity policies and procedures...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the use of compensating controls when encryption is infeasible.
evidence_quote: To the extent a covered entity determines that encryption of nonpublic information at rest is infeasible, the covered entity may instead secure such nonpublic information using effective alternative compensating controls that have been reviewed and approved by the covered entity's CISO in writing.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the use of compensating controls when multi-factor authentication is not feasible.
evidence_quote: If the covered entity has a CISO, the CISO may approve in writing the use of reasonably equivalent or more secure compensating controls. Such controls shall be reviewed periodically, but at a minimum annually.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
